informal: Show that $\sum^{\prime} 1 / n$, the sum being over square free integers, diverges.
lean:
theorem Ireland_Rosen_exercise_2_27a :
  ¬ Summable (λ i : {p : ℤ // Squarefree p} => (1 : ℚ) / i)
reasoning: The informal claim states that the sum of 1/n over (positive) squarefree integers diverges. The Lean encoding asserts that 1/i is not Summable over the subtype of squarefree integers in ℤ. Although the Lean version ranges over all of ℤ (including negative squarefree integers and ±1), this does not change the mathematical content: Summable in this context is equivalent to absolute (unconditional) summability, and the squarefree integers in ℤ are just ±(squarefree positive ℕ), so Σ |1/i| over squarefree ℤ equals 2·Σ_{n squarefree, n≥1} 1/n. Thus ¬Summable over squarefree ℤ holds iff the informal series diverges. The use of ℚ as the codomain doesn't matter for the (non-)summability claim. So the encoding faithfully captures the divergence statement.
